walkthrough.title: Board Layout
walkthrough.text: Let's get Wingspan set up! First, grab the bird deck, the bird tray (that's the card-holder with the lid), and the bonus deck. Set them in the middle of the table where everyone can reach. Let me know when you're ready.
walkthrough.image_caption: Bird deck, bonus deck, and bird tray

walkthrough.title: Bird Tray
walkthrough.text: Place both the bird deck and bonus deck face-down. Now draw 3 birds from the top of the bird deck and lay them face-up on the lid of the bird tray. This is the 'bird market' where players can shop for birds during the game.
walkthrough.image_caption: 3 face-up birds on the tray lid

walkthrough.title: Food Supply
walkthrough.text: Find the birdfeeder dice tower and the 5 food dice. Drop all 5 dice into the birdfeeder — just toss them in from the top and let them tumble down. The faces showing are the available food for this round.
walkthrough.image_caption: Birdfeeder dice tower with 5 food dice

walkthrough.title: Player Setup
walkthrough.text: Each player gets: one player mat, 8 action cubes of their color, 5 random bird cards from the deck, and 5 food tokens (one of each type). You'll discard some of these in a moment, but grab them all for now.

summary.title: Board Layout
bullets: Place bird deck face down
Place bonus deck face down
Draw 3 birds face-up on tray lid

summary.title: Bird Tray & Food
bullets: Set bird tray in center of table
Drop 5 food dice into birdfeeder tower
Dice faces showing = available food

summary.title: Player Setup
bullets: Each player: 1 player mat, 8 action cubes
Deal 5 bird cards + 5 food tokens (1 each type)
Players choose birds/food to keep (discard rest)

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you'll do exactly one of four actions: play a bird, gain food, lay eggs, or draw bird cards. You pick one action by placing an action cube on the leftmost open space of that row on your player mat.

walkthrough.title: Play a Bird
walkthrough.text: To play a bird from your hand, pay its food cost shown in the top-left corner. Then place the bird card in the leftmost open space of its matching habitat row — forest, grassland, or wetland. Some spaces also cost eggs.

walkthrough.title: Gain Food
walkthrough.text: Place your action cube on the gain food row. Take food dice from the birdfeeder matching your choice. Then slide your cube right, activating any bird powers in that row as you go. Forest birds often have food-related powers!

walkthrough.title: End of Round
walkthrough.text: After all players use their action cubes, the round ends. Remove one cube from the game — you'll have fewer actions each round, so make them count! Score the round's bonus, refill the bird tray, and start the next round.

summary.title: Turn Actions
bullets: Play a bird — pay food cost, place in matching habitat
Gain food — take dice from birdfeeder
Lay eggs — place eggs on birds with open egg slots
Draw bird cards — from face-up tray or deck

summary.title: Bird Powers
bullets: When activated (brown) — triggers when you use that row
Once between turns (pink) — triggers on other players' turns
Game end (teal) — bonus points at final scoring

summary.title: End of Round
bullets: All action cubes used = round over
Remove 1 cube from game (fewer actions next round)
Score round bonus, refill bird tray
4 rounds total, then final scoring

walkthrough.title: Early Game Focus
walkthrough.text: In rounds 1 and 2, prioritize getting birds on your mat. The more birds you have, the more powerful your engine becomes. Don't worry too much about eggs early — you need birds first to put eggs on!

walkthrough.title: Engine Building
walkthrough.text: Try to build a strong row. If your forest row has birds that generate extra food, keep adding forest birds. Each time you gain food, you'll trigger all those bird powers in sequence. That's the engine!

walkthrough.title: Late Game Scoring
walkthrough.text: In rounds 3 and 4, shift to scoring. Lay lots of eggs — they're worth a point each. Also check the end-of-round bonus goals and make sure you're competitive. Don't forget tucked and cached cards count too!

summary.title: Early Game
bullets: Rounds 1–2: focus on playing birds
Build a strong engine in one habitat row
Don't over-invest in eggs early

summary.title: Mid & Late Game
bullets: Rounds 3–4: shift to egg laying for points
Watch end-of-round bonus goals
Tucked/cached cards = extra points

summary.title: General Tips
bullets: Birds with low food costs are efficient early plays
Pink powers are strong in multiplayer
Count your points vs opponents before final round

entries.definition: The dice tower holding food dice. When gaining food, take dice from here. When empty or all showing same face, reroll all dice.
Your player mat has 3 rows: Forest (food), Grassland (eggs), Wetland (cards). Each bird goes in its matching habitat.
Activate when you use that habitat's action. Slide your action cube right to trigger each bird's brown power in order.
Trigger once between your turns, during other players' turns. Great in multiplayer games with more triggers.
Score bonus points at end of game based on conditions (e.g., number of birds in a habitat).
Some bird powers let you 'tuck' cards from the deck under that bird. Each tucked card = 1 point at game end.
Some bird powers let you 'cache' food tokens on that bird. Each cached food = 1 point at game end.
When activated, roll a die. If the result matches the bird's requirement, cache one food from the supply on this bird.
